title: Ecommerce systems and methods for purchasing gifts and parts of gifts using crowdfunding methodologies and social media platforms
abstract: A computer-implemented method of purchasing gifts includes under control of one or more configured computing systems, selecting a gift item (703) desired by a gift recipient, maintaining a list for the gift recipient that includes the selected gift item, and assigning a sales price to the selected gift item on the list. On a visual display, a processor is used for dividing the selected gift item into two or more gift parts that are available for individual purchase by gift contributors. The selected gift item is divided into the two or more gift parts on the visual display by showing a boundary (751) on the visual display that defines a bounded area, showing the selected gift item (703) on the visual display, inside the bounded area and surrounded by the boundary (751), and generating at least one dividing line (752, 753) inside the bounded area that divides the bounded area into two or more individual sections (704-731) that are visible on the visual display, whereby each of the gift parts is located inside a different one of the individual sections (704-731). A monetary value is assigned to each of the respective gift parts that is less than the sales price assigned to the selected gift item. Gift contributors can purchase at least one of the gift parts at the monetary value assigned thereto and leave comments that are unique to the gift part.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1 . A computer-implemented method of purchasing gifts comprising: under control of one or more configured computing systems, selecting a gift item desired by a gift recipient; maintaining a list for the gift recipient that includes the selected gift item; assigning a sales price to the selected gift item on the list; on a visual display, using a processor for dividing the selected gift item into two or more gift parts that are available for individual purchase by gift contributors; the selected gift item being divided into the two or more gift parts on the visual display by showing a boundary on the visual display that defines a bounded area, showing the selected gift item on the visual display, inside the bounded area and surrounded by the boundary, generating at least one dividing line inside the bounded area that divides the bounded area into two or more individual sections that are visible on the visual display, wherein each of the gift parts is located inside a different one of the individual sections; assigning a monetary value to each of the respective gift parts that is less than the sales price assigned to the selected gift item; receiving from at least one of the gift contributors a communication for purchasing at least one of the gift parts at the monetary value assigned thereto. 2. The method as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the showing the selected gift item on the visual display comprises showing an image of the selected gift item on the visual display, and wherein the image is selected from the group consisting of photographs, artwork, and text. 3. The method as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the boundary shown on the visual display comprises one or more boundary lines that define a shape selected from the group consisting of squares, rectangles, parallelograms, triangles, circles, ovals, objects having at least one curved line, and objects having at least one straight line. 4. The method as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the at least one dividing line comprises lines selected from the group consisting of horizontal lines, vertical lines, diagonal lines, curved arcs, straight lines, squiggly lines, and lines having the appearance of puzzle pieces. 5. The method as claimed in claim 1 , further comprising for each of the gift parts located inside a different one of the individual sections showing an available indicator when the gift part is available for purchase and an unavailable indicator when the gift part has been purchased and is unavailable for purchase. 6. The method as claimed in claim 5, wherein the available indicator has a different visual appearance than the unavailable indicator. 7. The method as claimed in claim 1 , further comprising: selecting one of the gift parts shown on the visual display; showing on the visual display the cost to purchase the selected gift part and a selector button to purchase the selected gift part. 8. The method as claimed in claim 7, further comprising: using the selector button to purchase the selected gift part; posting a message that is unique to the purchased gift part; linking the posted message to at least one social media platform or one or more social media networks. 9. The method as claimed in claim 1 , further comprising: summing the monetary values of the purchased gift parts of the selected gift item; and executing a buy order for the selected gift item when the sum of the monetary values of the purchased gift parts equals or is greater than the sales price to the gift item. 10. The method as claimed in claim 1 , further comprising notifying the gift recipient when at least one of the gift parts has been purchased by one of the gift contributors. 1 1. The method as claimed in claim 1 , further comprising using communication protocols selected from the group consisting of homepages, pages, social media platforms, social media networks, e-mail, text messaging, facsimile communications, printed communications, and written communications to notify the gift contributors of the selected gift item desired by the gift 

 recipient, wherein the gift contributors include individuals who have purchased a gift part desired by the gift recipient and potential gift contributors. 12. The method as claimed in claim 1 , further comprising: notifying retailers of the selected gift item; obtaining best selling price offers for the selected gift item from the retailers; using a lowest one of the best selling price offers from the retailers as the sales price assigned to the selected gift item. 13. The method as claimed in claim 1 , further comprising adding user selected ornamentation to the visual display that shows the selected gift item. 14. The method as claimed in claim 1 , further comprising: selecting a second gift item desired by the gift recipient; adding the second gift item to the list; assigning a priority rank order to the first and second gift items on the list to identify a higher ranked gift item and a lower ranked gift item; dividing the second gift item into one or more gift parts that are available for individual purchase by the gift contributors; authorizing purchase of the one or more gift parts of the lower ranked gift item only after all of the gift parts of the higher ranked gift item have been purchased. 15. The method as claimed in claim 1 , further comprising: showing a first side of the selected gift item on the visual display, wherein the first side of the selected gift item has the boundary that defines the bounded area; showing a second side of the selected gift item on the visual display, wherein the second side of the selected gift item has a second boundary that defines a second bounded area. 16. The method as claimed in claim 15, further comprising: showing at least a second portion of the selected gift item inside the second bounded area; generating at least one second dividing line inside the second bounded area that divides the second bounded area into two or more second side individual sections that are visible on 

 the visual display, wherein each of the gift parts shown on the second side of the selected gift item is located inside a different one of the second side individual sections. 17. A computer-implemented method of purchasing gifts comprising: under control of one or more configured computing systems, selecting two or more gift items desired by a gift recipient; assigning a highest to lowest rank order for the selected two or more gift items to generate a prioritized list; maintaining the prioritized list for the gift recipient listing the selected two or more gift items; assigning a sales price to each of the selected two or more gift items on the prioritized list; dividing each of the selected two or more gift items into two or more gift parts that are available for individual purchase by gift contributors; each of the selected gift items being divided into the two or more gift parts on the visual display by showing a boundary on the visual display that defines a bounded area, showing the selected gift item on the visual display and inside the bounded area, generating at least one dividing line inside the bounded area that divides the bounded area into two or more individual sections that are visible on the visual display, wherein each of the gift parts is located inside a different one of the individual sections; assigning a monetary value to each of the gift parts for each of the selected two or more gift items that is less than the sales price assigned to each of the selected two or more gift items; receiving from at least one of the gift contributors a communication for purchasing at least one of the gift parts at the monetary value assigned thereto. 18. The method as claimed in claim 17, further comprising authorizing purchase of the gift parts of a lower ranked gift item on the prioritized list only after all of the gift parts of a higher ranked gift item on the prioritized list have been purchased. 19. The method as claimed in claim 18, after confirming that the sum of the monetary values assigned to the purchased gift parts of the higher ranked gift item equals the sales price of the higher ranked gift item, executing a purchase order for the higher ranked gift item. 20. The method as claimed in claim 17, further comprising: displaying the selected two or more gift items on the visual display; linking the selected two or more gift items to one or more social media networks; enabling the gift recipient, the gift contributors, retailers, and third party users to generate comments that are uniquely related to each of the respective gift parts of the selected two or more gift items; transmitting the generated comments that are uniquely related to each of the respective gift parts of the selected two or more gift items to the one or more social media networks. 21. A computer-implemented method of purchasing items comprising: under control of one or more configured computing systems, creating a list having items that are sold by retailers; locking together any of the items on the list that are sold by a first retailer; prioritizing the items on the list from a highest ranked item to a lowest ranked item, wherein the items on the list that are locked together by the first retailer move together up and down the list as the list is re-prioritized. 22. The method as claimed in claim 21 , further comprising: locking together any of the items on the list that are sold by a second retailer, wherein the items on the list that are locked together by the second retailer move together up and down the list as the list is re-prioritized. 23. The method as claimed in claim 22, further comprising: creating a second list having items that are sold by retailers; moving the items that are locked together by the first retailer from the first list to the second list. 24. The method as claimed in claim 23, prioritizing the items on the second list from a highest ranked item to a lowest ranked item, wherein the items on the list that are locked 

 together by the first retailer move together up and down the second list as the second list is re- prioritized. 24. The method as claimed in claim 21 , further comprising: creating multiple lists, each of the multiple lists having items that are sold by retailers; for each list, locking together the items on the each list that are sold by a single retailer; moving the locked items from the first list to a second list, wherein the locked items from the first list remain together as they are moved from the first list to the second list; moving the locked items from the second list to the first list, wherein the locked items from the second list remain together as they are moved from the second list to the first list. 25. The method as claimed in claim 24, further comprising after moving the locked items on the first list to the second list, re-prioritizing the order of the items on the second list, wherein the locked items that have been moved from the first list to the second list move together up and down in priority as the second list is re-prioritized. 26. The method as claimed in claim 21 , further comprising: purchasing the items on the list that are locked together by the first retailer; and shipping the items on the list that are locked together by the first retailer jointly to a purchaser. 27. A computer-implemented method of purchasing items comprising: under control of one or more configured computing systems, creating a list having items that are sold by retailers; sorting the list so that the items on the list that are sold by a common retailer are located adjacent one another on the list; locking the sorted list so that the items that are sold by the common retailer are locked together in a locked group; prioritizing the items on the list from a highest ranked item to a lowest ranked item, wherein the items on the list that are locked together in the locked group move together up and down the list as the list is re-prioritized. 28. The method as claimed in claim 27, further comprising moving the locked group from the first list to a second list, wherein the items in the locked group move together between the first list and the second list. 29. The method as claimed in claim 28, wherein the locked group is moveable automatically using code or manually by dragging the locked group from the first list to the second list, and wherein the locked group is moveable up and down the list automatically using code or manually by dragging the locked group. 30. A gifting system comprising: one or more configured computing systems; a gift application executable in the one or more configured computing systems, the gift application comprising code for selecting a gift item; code for maintaining a list that includes the selected gift item; code for assigning a sales price to the selected gift item on the list; code for showing the selected gift item on a visual display; code for showing a first side of the selected gift on the visual display, wherein the first side of the selected gift item has a first boundary that defines a first bounded area; code for showing at least a first portion of the selected gift item inside the first bounded area; code for generating at least one first dividing line inside the first bounded area that divides the first bounded area into two or more first side individual sections that are visible on the visual display, wherein each of the gift parts shown on the first side of the selected gift is located inside a different one of the first side individual sections code for assigning a monetary value to each of the respective gift parts that is less than the sales price assigned to the selected gift item; and code receiving from at least one of the gift contributors a communication for purchasing at least one of the gift parts at the monetary value assigned thereto. 31. The system as claimed in claim 30, further comprising: 

 code for showing a second side of the selected gift on the visual display, wherein the second side of the selected gift item has a second boundary that defines a second bounded area; code for showing at least a second portion of the selected gift item inside the second bounded area; code for generating at least one second dividing line inside the second bounded area that divides the second bounded area into two or more second side individual sections that are visible on the visual display, wherein each of the gift parts shown on the second side of the selected gift is located inside a different one of the second side individual sections. 32. The system as claimed in claim 31 , wherein the gift application executable in the one or more configured computing systems further comprises logic for visually distinguishing the gift parts that are available for purchase from the gift parts that are unavailable for purchase.
